text.title: Helm Chart Reliability and Security
text.summary: Production checklist: HPA, PodDisruptionBudget, anti-affinity, health probes, and non-root security context — the gaps found in 83% of popular charts.

rule.statement: Set HPA minReplicas >= 2. Never set minReplicas to 1 for production services.
rule.rationale: minReplicas=1 defeats high availability: when the single pod is evicted or the node is drained, the service has zero replicas until HPA reacts.

rule.statement: Define PodDisruptionBudget with minAvailable=2 (or maxUnavailable=1) for all stateless services.
rule.rationale: Without PDB, node drain during upgrades can evict all replicas simultaneously causing outage. PDB prevents the cluster from removing too many pods at once.

rule.statement: Configure pod anti-affinity (soft by default, hard for critical services) to spread pods across nodes and zones.
rule.rationale: Without anti-affinity, the scheduler may place all replicas on one node; a node failure takes the entire service down.

rule.statement: Define all three probes: livenessProbe, readinessProbe, and startupProbe for slow-starting applications.
rule.rationale: livenessProbe restarts deadlocked containers. readinessProbe prevents traffic to pods not yet ready. startupProbe prevents liveness from killing slow-start containers.

rule.statement: Always define resource requests and limits. Use configChecksum annotations to trigger pod restart when ConfigMap or Secret changes.
rule.rationale: Missing requests break scheduling decisions. Without checksum annotations, config changes require manual rollout.

rule.statement: Set runAsNonRoot: true, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false, and drop ALL capabilities in securityContext.
rule.rationale: Root containers can escape to the host. Read-only filesystem prevents runtime modification. Capability drop minimizes attack surface.

example.code: securityContext: allowPrivilegeEscalation: false readOnlyRootFilesystem: true runAsNonRoot: true runAsUser: 1000 capabilities: drop: - ALL

rule.statement: Enable NetworkPolicy to restrict ingress to only ingress-controller and same-namespace pods. Allow egress for DNS (UDP 53) and same-namespace pods.
rule.rationale: Without NetworkPolicy, any compromised pod in the cluster can reach your service directly, bypassing ingress rules.

rule.statement: Create a dedicated ServiceAccount with automountServiceAccountToken: false unless the application needs the K8s API.
rule.rationale: Default service account token is mounted automatically and can be used for cluster API access if the container is compromised.

rule.statement: Always use --atomic flag for helm upgrade in CI/CD. Set --timeout appropriately. Configure rolling update strategy.
rule.rationale: --atomic rolls back automatically on failure, preventing partial upgrades. Without it, a failed upgrade leaves the release in a broken state.

antipattern.description: Using helm upgrade without --atomic in CI/CD.
antipattern.reason: A failed upgrade without --atomic leaves the release in a degraded state requiring manual intervention. --atomic ensures clean rollback.
